original_text:
### Iconography
- Style: **minimal line illustration** for clarity and quick recall. New icons are permitted but **must match this stylistic approach** (consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).
- Icon colors and backgrounds adjustable **only within the brand palette**.
- Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. [GENERAL: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.]

relations.note: Both GENERAL email icon requirements support low-vision legibility.

quotes: Style: **minimal line illustration** for clarity and quick recall. New icons are permitted but **must match this stylistic approach** (consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).

Style: **minimal line illustration** for clarity and quick recall. New icons are permitted but **must match this stylistic approach** (consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).
Icon colors and backgrounds adjustable **only within the brand palette**.

rules.intent: Maintain instantly recognizable, coherent brand iconography.
rules.rule_text: Icons, including newly created icons, must use the minimal line-illustration style with consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, and single-color line work. Icon colors and backgrounds may be adjusted only within the brand palette.

quotes: Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. [GENERAL: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.]

rules.intent: Keep icons legible for low-vision audiences.
rules.rule_text: For low-vision accessibility, email icons must render at the minimum 32×32px size.

quotes: default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds
Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. [GENERAL: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.]

rules.intent: Provide a consistent, high-contrast default icon treatment.
rules.rule_text: On light backgrounds in email, use default Brand Blue (#00529b) icon strokes to support contrast for low-vision audiences.
